name: Classic French Fries with Chili Salt
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine catsup, cumin, and vinegar.
2. In another small bowl, mix salt and chili powder.
(This can be prepared up to 1 day in advance. Cover each mixture separately and let stand at room temperature.)
3. Place the potatoes on a kitchen towel in parallel rows.
4. Roll the towel tightly around the potatoes, enclosing them completely, and let stand for at least 30 minutes up to 1 hour to dry.
5. Heat oil in a heavy large saucepan to a depth of 3 inches. Attach a deep-fry thermometer and heat the oil over medium-high heat to 320°F (160°C).
6. Add one-quarter of the potatoes to the hot oil and fry until just tender and barely colored, about 3 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, transfer the potatoes to a wire rack set over paper towels and allow them to drain.
7. Preheat the oil to 320°F (160°C) if necessary and repeat frying with the remaining potatoes in three more batches.
8. Cool the fried potatoes completely. This can be prepared up to 3 hours in advance; let the potatoes stand at room temperature, and cool the oil as needed.
9. Reheat the oil to 400°F (200°C) and fry the potatoes in three batches until they are deep golden brown and beginning to develop a crust, about 2 minutes.
10. Using a slotted spoon, transfer the potatoes to a paper towel-lined basket. Sprinkle with chili salt and serve with cumin catsup.
